baseline.description: Direct spatial mapping with one MAC-equivalent DSP per grid position.

dsp.source_desc: Baseline spatial mapping uses one DSP-equivalent per MAC position.

lut.source_desc: Lightweight linear LUT envelope used to compare architectures on a common normalized basis.

wns_estimate_ns.source_desc: Simple timing-risk trend model used only when no measured timing anchor exists.

execution.formula: capacity_scale, latency_penalty_fraction, and fixed_phase_overhead_cycles come from architecture semantics
execution.source_desc: Baseline has no sharing penalty and serves as the reference execution model.

baseline.scope_note: Framework baseline rows refer to the modelled one-MAC-per-position baseline family. Direct baseline slice measurements calibrate this family more strongly than the other architecture families.

shared.description: Time-multiplexed compute that reduces DSP demand at the cost of control/latency overhead.

dsp.source_desc: Shared mode assumes two logical MAC positions are time-multiplexed onto one DSP-equivalent resource.

lut.source_desc: Shared control overhead raises the fixed term while reducing per-MAC datapath LUT cost.

wns_estimate_ns.source_desc: Shared mode timing estimate follows the same simple trend model with a slightly lower bias.

execution.formula: capacity_scale, latency_penalty_fraction, and fixed_phase_overhead_cycles come from sharing semantics
execution.source_desc: Shared mode keeps nominal spatial capacity but adds replay/control overhead that inflates phase latency.

shared.scope_note: Framework shared rows refer to a modelled DSP-reducing shared family. The directly measured 4x4 shared direct slice is a separate implementation-specific observation and does not validate global DSP reduction.

replicated.description: Duplicated compute intended to preserve throughput at higher resource cost.

dsp.source_desc: Replicated mode duplicates compute lanes, doubling DSP-equivalent demand relative to baseline.

lut.source_desc: Replicated mode carries a larger fixed and per-MAC control/data-path cost envelope.

wns_estimate_ns.source_desc: Replicated mode is assigned the weakest timing bias because routing pressure grows with duplicated compute.

execution.formula: capacity_scale, latency_penalty_fraction, and fixed_phase_overhead_cycles come from replication semantics
execution.source_desc: Replicated mode doubles effective capacity while keeping only a small coordination overhead.

replicated.scope_note: Framework replicated rows refer to the modelled duplicated-compute family and remain analytical except where anchored failure evidence is present.

field_overrides.source_desc: Canonical prior MAC-array study anchor for baseline 8x8 DSP usage.
field_overrides.derivation: direct anchored fact carried forward from earlier static architecture study

field_overrides.source_desc: Canonical prior MAC-array study anchor showing 8x8 shared halves DSP demand.
field_overrides.derivation: direct anchored fact carried forward from earlier static architecture study

field_overrides.value: Prior study anchor: 8x8 shared reduces DSP from 64 to 32 with only a small latency increase.
field_overrides.source_desc: Qualitative static-study note preserved for interpretability.
Prior study anchor preserving Artix-7 implementation failure for 8x8 replicated.
field_overrides.derivation: direct anchored fact carried forward from earlier static architecture study

field_overrides.source_desc: Negative timing anchor used to preserve 8x8 replicated timing failure evidence.
field_overrides.derivation: anchored timing indicator carried forward from earlier static architecture study

field_overrides.source_desc: Direct 4x4 shared slice measurement on Artix-7 exists and is implementation-specific.
field_overrides.derivation: summarized from direct 4x4 baseline-vs-shared measured tradeoff artifact

field_overrides.value: Direct 4x4 shared slice measurement is implementation-specific: DSP stays at 16 like baseline, LUT drops to 679, and latency increases from 33 to 65 cycles.
field_overrides.source_desc: Direct 4x4 shared slice tradeoff measured through Vivado and Verilator.

default_components.name: phase_boundary_quiesce
default_components.source_desc: Controller drains the current phase and quiesces outstanding work.

default_components.name: mode_select_and_state_programming
default_components.source_desc: Mode-select/control state is updated before the next phase starts.

default_components.name: scratch_state_warmup
default_components.source_desc: Buffers and counters are warmed for the next mode at phase boundaries.

pair_overrides.source_desc: Shared->baseline requires less warmup than shared->replicated.

components.name: phase_boundary_quiesce
components.source_desc: Controller drains the previous shared phase.

components.name: mode_select_and_state_programming
components.source_desc: Select baseline mode and program control state.

pair_overrides.source_desc: Shared->replicated requires extra warmup to activate duplicated lanes.

components.name: phase_boundary_quiesce
components.source_desc: Controller drains the previous shared phase.

components.name: mode_select_and_state_programming
components.source_desc: Select replicated mode and program lane control state.

components.name: scratch_state_warmup
components.source_desc: Warm duplicated execution lanes and counters.
